witnesses.description: A feasible s-t flow of value 3 on a four-node directed network.
The source-side cut {s} has capacity 3 and is saturated by the feasible flow.
A malformed flow sends 3 units on an edge whose capacity is 2.

checks.claim: The listed directed flow is feasible and has value 3.
checks.notes: The validator checks nonnegative capacities and flows, every flow <= capacity, conservation at internal vertices, and source/sink balance.

checks.claim: The listed feasible flow saturates an s-t cut of capacity 3.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the directed cut capacity and net cut flow. Equality between flow value and cut capacity certifies optimality for this finite instance only.

checks.claim: The malformed flow respects every edge capacity.
checks.notes: The validator locates the listed edge s->a and rejects the row because the claimed flow 3 exceeds capacity 2.

checks.claim: The same network has a feasible s-t flow of value 4.
checks.notes: Exact cut replay computes capacity 3 for the source-side cut, so a claimed feasible flow value 4 violates the finite cut upper bound.

checks.claim: There is a feasible s-t flow with value 4 while the listed source-side cut has capacity 3.

data.certificate: produce_lra_evidence emits Evidence::UnsatFarkas checked by Evidence::check for the exact rational inequality conflict 4 <= 3

checks.notes: The SMT-LIB artifact isolates the finite cut-bound contradiction after replay computes the cut capacity.

checks.claim: The general max-flow/min-cut theorem for arbitrary finite networks requires future theorem-prover reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: For every finite capacitated s-t network, the maximum feasible flow value equals the minimum s-t cut capacity.
data.future_checker: Lean proof of the theorem plus a certificate bridge for algorithm output, integrality, and optional min-cost-flow extensions.

checks.notes: The checked rows cover one explicit network and one explicit cut certificate. They do not prove the general theorem or algorithm correctness.
